svg.text: RFID Optimus Encoder — Board Schematic Overview (block/net level)
Reference designators match BOM.md. Exact pin numbers: pinout.md. Not a KiCad export — a hand-authored reference diagram (see hardware/README.md).

g.text: D1/D2 = SS14 Schottky OR-ing so USB-C and the DC input never back-feed each other.

svg.text: 3V3 → U1, U2, U5, J3 (feeds every logic IC on the board)

g.text: to EN (SW1 reset also here) and GPIO0 (BOOT_SW also here)
Antenna matching/tuning lives on the plugged-in module, not this board — see README "Why a pluggable PN532 module."
Shares SPI_SCK/MISO/MOSI bus with J3 — separate CS lines, same bus.
J6: NO / COM / NC → external lock
Not on a 1-off prototype for yourself — added because this board ships to
someone else's install, wired by someone who isn't you. See ../PRODUCTION.md.
U8 MCP73871 — charge management + power-path control
charges BATT1 from the 5V rail when present; SYS_OUT powers the board
from {input, battery} automatically, no firmware involvement
reads BATT1 directly, reports SOC% + voltage over I2C
a gauge only — U7/Q4 above are what actually protect the cell
I2C (GPIO21/22, BATT_SDA/BATT_SCL) to U1 — see BatteryMonitor in firmware

svg.text: Full exact pin-for-pin mapping: pinout.md. Parts + reference designators: BOM.md.
This is a hand-authored reference diagram for recreating in KiCad/Eagle — see hardware/README.md for why (no EDA tooling available in the environment this was written in).
